visa_policy.notes: Kosovo's visa policy has been actively evolving since 2024. Kosovo abolished visas for Bosnia and Herzegovina effective January 1, 2025 (biometric ID card required). The UAE–Kosovo MoU entered into force February 22, 2024. Kosovo removed Botswana, Namibia, and South Africa from the visa-free list in August 2024 as part of EU alignment commitments. Russia ordinary passport holders require a visa; only Russian diplomatic/service passport holders have a 15-day conditional exemption. Taiwan is technically exempt but requires 2-week advance notification to a Kosovo diplomatic mission — recorded under conditional_access. The archived MFA site (arkivi.mfa-ks.net) remains the primary authoritative list; the live site (mfa-ks.net) does not publish an English visa list. Some newly added countries (Bahrain, Oman, Qatar, Eswatini, Guyana, Papua New Guinea, Sao Tome and Principe) are referenced by Wikipedia and EU Commission reporting but not yet verifiable from the official Kosovo MFA archive page.

cbi.notes: Kosovo does not operate a citizenship-by-investment programme. No official government source documents any such program. Kosovo's EU accession aspirations are cited as a key reason for not establishing CBI.

programs.name: Temporary Residence Permit for Business/Employment (Entrepreneur Route)
programs.notes: Foreign nationals who register and operate a business in Kosovo may apply for a temporary residence permit for employment/business purposes under Law 08/L-296 on Foreigners. No minimum investment amount is set by law. Continuous 5 years of temporary residence leads to permanent residence eligibility. No golden visa or investor-specific fast-track exists.

credential.subtype: biometric residence permit (any Schengen member state)

conditional_access.conditions: Citizen of a country required to obtain a Kosovo visa; must hold a valid biometric residence permit issued by one of the Schengen member states; stay limited to 15 days.
Citizen of a country required to obtain a Kosovo visa; must hold a valid multi-entry Schengen Visa; stay limited to 15 days.

credential.issuer: EU | Schengen | UK | US | Canada | Australia | Japan
credential.subtype: refugee or stateless person travel document (1951 Refugee Convention / 1954 Stateless Persons Convention) or valid travel document for foreigners

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of travel documents issued by EU Member States, Schengen zone States, United Kingdom, United States of America, Canada, Australia or Japan based on the 1951 Convention on Refugee Status or the 1954 Convention on the Status of Stateless Persons, as well as holders of valid travel documents for foreigners, may enter, pass through the territory and stay for up to 15 days without a visa.

credential.issuer: UN | NATO | OSCE | Council of Europe | EU
credential.subtype: Laissez-Passer (issued by UN Organizations, NATO, OSCE, Council of Europe, or European Union)

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of Laissez-Passer, regardless of their nationality, issued by United Nations Organizations, NATO, OSCE, Council of Europe and European Union are exempt from the visa requirement.

Holders of diplomatic and service passports issued by the Russian Federation, People's Republic of China, Arab Republic of Egypt, Republic of Indonesia, and Ukraine may enter, transit or stay up to 15 days in Kosovo. Note: ordinary passport holders from Russia and China require a visa.

Taiwan passport holders are exempt from the visa requirement but must provide preliminary notification to a Kosovo diplomatic or consular mission at least two weeks before travel. This advance-notification requirement means entry is not purely nationality-based.

validation_notes: REMOVED Russia (RUS) from visa_free: The archived Kosovo MFA list included Russia on the ordinary-passport exempt list, but this was an error — official sources confirm only Russian diplomatic/service passport holders get a 15-day exemption; ordinary Russian passport holders require a visa. Russia remains only in conditional_access.
REMOVED Botswana (BWA), Namibia (NAM), South Africa (ZAF) from visa_free: Kosovo reinstated visa requirements for these three countries in August 2024 as part of EU visa-policy alignment commitments.

MOVED Taiwan (TWN) from visa_free to conditional_access: The archived MFA page shows Taiwan is exempt only with mandatory 2-week advance notification to a Kosovo diplomatic mission — this is a conditional requirement, not a pure nationality-based waiver.

ADDED Bosnia and Herzegovina (BIH) to visa_free: Kosovo unilaterally abolished visa requirement effective January 1, 2025, under the Agreement on Free Movement with ID Cards in the Western Balkans; biometric ID card required.

ADDED United Arab Emirates (ARE) to visa_free: Kosovo–UAE bilateral MoU signed January 11, 2024 entered into force February 22, 2024 (UAE MOFA official source); 90 days per visit, covers ordinary, diplomatic, special and mission passports.

ADDED Bahrain (BHR), Oman (OMN), Qatar (QAT) to visa_free: Referenced in Wikipedia (Visa policy of Kosovo) and EU Commission visa-alignment reporting (December 2025 SWD) as countries Kosovo has granted visa-free access but the EU has not; not yet confirmed on the official Kosovo MFA archived list.

ADDED Eswatini (SWZ), Guyana (GUY), Papua New Guinea (PNG), Sao Tome and Principe (STP) to visa_free: Referenced on Wikipedia and embassies.net as visa-free; not confirmed on official Kosovo MFA archived page.
ADDED Taiwan conditional_access entry: Moved from visa_free into conditional_access with basis=special_status to correctly reflect the mandatory advance-notification condition.
UPDATED conditional_access Russia/China/Egypt/Indonesia/Ukraine note to explicitly state that Russia and China ordinary passport holders require a visa.
gaps: The live Kosovo MFA website (mfa-ks.net) does not publish a full English-language visa-free list; data sourced from the archived site (arkivi.mfa-ks.net) which may not reflect the most recent updates.
Kosovo does not appear to publish a publicly accessible e-visa portal; confirmation that no e-visa system exists could not be fully verified from an official source.

The Ministry of Internal Affairs site (mpb.rks-gov.net) was inaccessible during research (connection refused), so full details on residence permit types and investor categories could not be directly confirmed from that source.

Bahrain, Oman, Qatar, Eswatini, Guyana, Papua New Guinea, and Sao Tome and Principe are visa-free per Wikipedia and EU Commission reporting but not yet confirmable against the official Kosovo MFA archived page — marked source_official=false.

Kosovo has committed to removing at least one country per year from its non-EU-aligned visa-free list between 2025 and 2027; the specific countries to be removed are not yet announced and may affect the current list.
No official digital nomad visa, talent visa, or startup visa program was found documented on official Kosovo government sources.

The exact current status of Mongolia, Morocco, Vietnam, Yemen, Tunisia, Zambia, Zimbabwe for Kosovo entry could not be re-confirmed from a post-2024 official source — retained from the prior archived MFA page.
No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder exemption found on any official Kosovo government source.

US visa holders, UK visa holders, Canadian visa holders, or Australian visa holders (ordinary, non-refugee) are NOT listed as a conditional exemption on the official Kosovo MFA source; only Schengen residence permit and multi-entry Schengen visa grant a credential-based waiver.
Bosnia and Herzegovina biometric-ID-only entry rule (vs. passport) needs re-confirmation once the live Kosovo MFA site publishes an updated English-language list.

outbound_official.note: No official Kosovo government outbound visa-list page located. The Kosovo Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Diaspora (mfa-ks.net / arkivi.mfa-ks.net / ambasadat.net) does not publish a dedicated page listing countries where Kosovo ordinary-passport citizens can travel without a prior consular visa — analogous to India's mea.gov.in/vffin. The MFA publishes individual press releases about newly signed visa-free agreements (e.g. Thailand, UAE) but maintains no consolidated outbound destination list. The archived MFA consular-services section (arkivi.mfa-ks.net) covers only inbound visa policy for foreign nationals entering Kosovo. The EU Integration Office (integrimievropian.rks-gov.net) addresses Schengen liberalisation only. Rely on destination-side data and the inbound-inverted visa_policy section.
